parents.name: Itsko (Isaac) Lidkovskiy
parents.note: Confirmed — named on 1858 10th Revision List (NHABGrodno/24/7/478, p.242v) and as father on birth record of her brother Borukh (NHABGrodno/1/28/034). Death certificate lists father as 'Isaac Mitkows[ky]' — informant Isidore garbled the surname.

parents.name: Rokhlya (Rachel)
parents.note: Confirmed — listed as mother on birth record of Gussie's brother Borukh Lidkovskiy (NHABGrodno/1/28/034, p.848481). Maiden surname unknown.

spouses.name: Michael Hiner (Meilech Hynkavsky)
spouses.note: Carpenter. Never emigrated — almost certainly died in Slonim. No US records found for him. Confirmed as father via Benjamin's 1907 marriage certificate ('Michel Honkofsky') and Isidore's 1925 marriage certificate ('Milton' Hyner).

known_siblings.name: Borukh (Baruch) Lidkovskiy
known_siblings.note: Confirmed sibling — same father (Itsko) and mother (Rokhlya) on birth record NHABGrodno/1/28/034, p.848481. Born ~1 year after Gussie. Did not emigrate.

known_siblings.name: Aizik (Isaac) Litkovski
known_siblings.note: Probable sibling — listed as son of Itsko, residing in Slonim, in 1912 Grodno Gubernia Voters List (ID SLOa0476). Still in Slonim 1912. Did not emigrate. Likely perished in Holocaust (Slonim massacre September 1942).

known_siblings.name: Samuel Litcofsky (~1848)
known_siblings.note: UNCONFIRMED possible sibling — WikiTree profile Litcofsky-16. Right age to be Gussie's sibling. Needs census birthplace confirmation.

facts.place: Golynka (Holinka), Slonim District, Grodno Gubernia, Russian Empire (now Belarus)
facts.place_note: Golynka confirmed as family home town from 1858 revision list and 1852 birth record of her brother Borukh. Same village as her future husband Michael Hynkavsky.
facts.source: NYC Death Certificate Reg No. 3460 (age at death); JewishGen Belarus Database 1858 revision list NHABGrodno/24/7/478; birth record of sibling NHABGrodno/1/28/034

facts.summary: Father: Itsko (Isaac) Lidkovskiy, b. ~1823, Golynka. Grandfather: Khaim Lidkovskiy. Mother: Rokhlya (Rachel), maiden surname unknown.
facts.archive_revision_list: NHABGrodno/24/7/478, page 242v — 1858 10th Revision List (revizskie skazki), Golynka, Slonim. Entry: 'Itsko son of Khaim Lidkovskiy, b. ~1823.'
facts.archive_birth_record: NHABGrodno/1/28/034, page 848481 — 1852 birth record of Borukh Lidkovskiy: 'Father: Itsko Lidkovskiy. Mother: Rokhlya.' Both father and grandfather Khaim confirmed.

facts.place: Brooklyn (Kings County), New York — AD 22, ED 20, House #95
facts.note: Listed as 'Gussie Hyner', age 55, mother-in-law in household of son-in-law Ely Ruderman. Household also includes daughter Celie Ruderman, grandson Milton Ruderman, and sons Isidore Hyner (25) and Morris Hyner (20).
Listed as 'Gusie', mother, in son Isidore Hyner's household.

facts.source: NYC Department of Health Standard Certificate of Death, Registration No. 3460

certificate_details.father_note: Informant (son Isidore) garbled the surname — 'Mitkows[ky]' is a corruption of 'Lidkowsky/Litkowsky.' The first name Isaac = 'Itsko' is confirmed correct.
certificate_details.mother_note: Informant did not know mother's name — wrote the husband's surname 'Hyner' erroneously. Actual mother's name was Rokhlya (Rachel), confirmed from sibling birth record.

facts.place: Jewish Cemetery, Congregation Sons of Israel, Spring Valley, Rockland County, New York
facts.note: Burial arranged by son Isidore. Her son Morris 'Moe' Hyner is also buried at this cemetery (died 17 Dec 1920, age 25).

old_country_ancestors.note: Both the Litcofsky (Lidkovskiy) and Hynkavsky (Ginkovskiy) families lived in the same village — Golynka, Slonim. Michael and Gussie were neighbors before marriage.

paternal_grandfather.name: Khaim Lidkovskiy
paternal_grandfather.source: 1858 revision list NHABGrodno/24/7/478 p.242v; sibling birth record NHABGrodno/1/28/034 — both confirm 'son of Khaim'

mother.name: Rokhlya (Rachel)
mother.source: Birth record of son Borukh, NHABGrodno/1/28/034, page 848481

research_notes.note: Original dossier created from GEDCOM export. Very sparse — only birthplace 'Russia' was known.

Major update from Gussie's death certificate (NYC Reg No. 3460) and JewishGen Belarus Database research. Death cert gave father's name as 'Isaac Mitkows[ky]' — identified as corruption of 'Itsko Lidkovskiy'. Confirmed via JewishGen 1858 revision list (NHABGrodno/24/7/478) showing Itsko son of Khaim in Golynka, Slonim. Birth record of brother Borukh (NHABGrodno/1/28/034) additionally confirmed: father Itsko, grandfather Khaim, and revealed mother's name as Rokhlya (Rachel) — previously unknown.

Siblings identified: (1) Borukh Lidkovskiy, b. 2 Sep 1852, Golynka — confirmed from birth record. (2) Aizik Litkovski, son of Itsko, still in Slonim 1912 — from Grodno Gubernia Voters List. (3) Samuel Litcofsky (~1848, WikiTree Litcofsky-16) — possible sibling, unconfirmed.

Death certificate listed mother as 'Sarah Hyner' — this is an error by informant son Isidore, who did not know his grandmother's name and wrote the family surname. Actual mother's name Rokhlya/Rachel is confirmed from archive record.

Both the Litcofsky and Hynkavsky families appear in the SAME archive file (NHABGrodno/24/7/478) for Golynka village. Gussie and Michael Hynkavsky grew up as neighbors in the same small village before marriage.

entries.note: Derechin is in Slonim district. Yizkor book shows Kalman Lidkovsky + wife Breineh + daughter perished there. Researcher likely inactive.

entries.researcher: Sari Laufer or Sari Rebecca Laufer
entries.note: Our town. Entry not assigned to a separate named researcher in the results — likely belongs to one of the Laufer researchers.
Vawkavysk (Volkovysk) is in Grodno region, near Slonim. Likely same extended family network.
Zelva is in Slonim district. Part of same regional cluster.
Halynka = Golynka = OUR VILLAGE. First of two Halynka entries.
Krasnapollye is in Mogilev Gubernia — a different region. May be a separate branch. However Alan was active as recently as January 2026 — worth contacting.
Gomel/Mogilev branch. The Belarus Births false-match record (Litkovskiy Mendel', born Gomel 1912) suggests there is a separate Gomel Litkovsky family. Researcher likely inactive.

*** MOST IMPORTANT CONTACT *** Halynka = Golynka = our exact village. Sari Rebecca Laufer is actively researching the Litkofsky family in the same village where Gussie, her father Itsko, grandfather Khaim, and brothers Borukh and Aizik all lived. Last logged in September 2023 — relatively recent. Almost certainly researching the same family branch. JewishGen contact link available (JGID #1023680). Note: May be related to / same person as Sari Laufer #21140 (both Laufer, both Litkofsky).

jgff_researchers.action_recommended: Contact Sari Rebecca Laufer (JGID #1023680) via JewishGen — she is researching Litkofsky in Halynka/Golynka, our exact village, and was active as recently as September 2023. Contact URL: https://www.jewishgen.org/jgff/jgffform.php (log in, search Litcofsky, click her contact link). Also consider contacting Alan Naselsky (#59444, Krasnapollye, active January 2026).
